title: boot-time I2C bus recovery before mag probe (gh #43 continued)
description: TezlaKid's latest log shows the bit-bang fix in 9e1dd43 doesn't help because runOnce never runs the recovery path — selectMagOnEitherBus() fails at boot (bus stuck from prior session), haveMag stays false, headingIsValid stays false forever, runOnce early-exits with 'return 500'. Calibration menu shows 'Magnetometer not detected.' Fix: extract bit-bang into static recoverI2cBus() helper, call it on both Wire and Wire1 at the top of selectMagOnEitherBus(), and refactor runOnce's recovery to use the same helper (DRY).
close_reason: Patch landed in 5eb543d. selectMagOnEitherBus now calls recoverI2cBus on both Wire and Wire1 before probing; runOnce recovery path refactored to share the helper. Build green. Awaiting TezlaKid retest.

title: bit-bang I2C bus recovery + all-zeros guard for QMC5883L (gh #43 continued)
description: PR #44's end()/begin() recovery is insufficient: it bounces the master TWIM but doesn't free a slave clamping SDA low. TezlaKid's log on PR #44 shows 'QMC write CTRL2 (soft reset) failed' repeating across recoveries — the bus is still hung when qmcInit() runs. Also: when the bus partially recovers, reads succeed but return x=y=z=0 (heading=360 stuck), and the streak counter resets on the false-success so recovery never fires again. Two fixes stacked on the same branch (ff-gh43/qmc-recovery): (1) bit-banged 9-SCL-clock unstick + START/STOP before magBus->end()/begin()/qmcInit() so the bus is actually free; (2) treat all-zero raw reads as failure so silent-stuck detection works.
close_reason: Patch landed in 9e1dd43; podman build green (+256B flash). PR #44 updated. Awaiting TezlaKid retest with bit-bang + all-zeros guard.

title: QMC re-init on consecutive failures (ff-gh43)
description: Per gh issue #43, log COM29_5.23.2026_9.40PM: Wire_nRF52 timeout fix (0415a00) prevents the freeze but leaves the QMC5883L in a stuck state after a TWIM force-reset. Mag spams 'QMC read failed; will retry.' indefinitely without recovery. Re-apply the reverted qmcFailCount logic from 68f6367 — now that qmcReadRaw returns false on Wire timeout, the counter actually advances and end()/begin()/qmcInit() runs to reconfigure the slave.
close_reason: Patch landed on ff-gh43/qmc-recovery in cdb2cc1; podman build green; PR #44 opened. Real-hardware confirmation pending TezlaKid field test on T114 with QMC5883L.

title: patch Wire_nRF52.cpp TWIM spin loops with millis() timeouts
description: Root cause of T114 freeze/crash loop per docs/wire-i2c-timeout-plan.md: bare TWIM spin loops in framework-arduinoadafruitnrf52/libraries/Wire/Wire_nRF52.cpp have no timeout. When TWIM peripheral gets stuck (LoRa RF noise → QMC5883L), no events fire, CPU spins forever, device freezes. Prior PR #41 (qmcFailCount in MagnetometerModule) lives above Wire and never runs because qmcReadRaw never returns. Reverted in bc92e0e. This issue: add a new patch block to patch-t114.py that injects millis()-based timeouts into requestFrom() and endTransmission(), with a TWIM force-reset helper, anchored on unique upstream signatures at SHA e13f5820002a4fb2a5e6754b42ace185277e5adf of meshtastic/Adafruit_nRF52_Arduino.
close_reason: Patch landed in 0415a00; podman build green; Wire_nRF52 timeouts compile into libFrameworkArduino.a; firmware.uf2 produced. Real-hardware confirmation still pending field test on T114 with mag attached.

title: Magnetometer I2C timeout: qmcReadRegs blocks forever on bus lockup
description: The QMC5883 magnetometer on the T114 shares the I2C1 bus. When radio TX corrupts the bus (noise during a 1009ms LoRa frame), the nRF52 TWI peripheral state machine gets stuck. qmcReadRegs() calls endTransmission()/requestFrom() with no timeout — these poll for a TWI event that never arrives and block forever, hanging the device. Observed in serial logs: 6 rapid 'QMC read failed; will retry.' entries (bus degraded but returning), then silence (7th call blocks indefinitely). Fix: add an I2C timeout to qmcReadRegs() via Wire1.setTimeout() or a TWI bus-reset recovery sequence on repeated failure. Implemented as a patch in patch-t114.py targeting MagnetometerModule.cpp.
close_reason: patch_mag_i2c_timeout() implemented and builds clean

title: Add 'Track' action to node list menu; read position from NodeDB (gh38)
description: Patch MenuHandler.cpp: add a 'Track' option to the node list menu (nodeListMenu). When selected, call friendFinderModule->startTracking(nodeNum) which enters TRACKING state and reads position from nodeDB->getMeshNode(nodeNum) on each render frame — no packets sent. Compass arrow updates whenever NodeDB receives a fresh position broadcast. Scope: patch-t114.py addition targeting nodeListMenu and the TRACKING state render path in FriendFinderModule. See gh issue #38 Change 1 acceptance criteria.
close_reason: Track action implemented in favoriteBaseMenu (not nodeListMenu — favBase has currentFavoriteNodeNum already set; node list uses a picker which doesn't carry the nodeNum). MENU_FAV_CB_NEW in patch-t114.py calls friendFinderModule->startTracking(currentFavoriteNodeNum).

title: Captain's Compass redesign: replace pairing protocol with NodeDB reads (gh38)
description: Remove the custom CAPABILITY_QUERY/PAIR_REQUEST/POSITION_UPDATE packet protocol and pairing FSM states from FriendFinderModule. Replace with direct NodeDB position reads. This eliminates the crash-after-pairing bug (root cause: custom packet stream interacting with mesh routing / PKC decrypt path) and removes the per-session consent friction. Tracking should work with any Meshtastic node, not just ones running Friend Finder firmware. See gh issue #38 Change 1.
close_reason: Implemented in patch-t114.py patch_compass_redesign(): pairing functions removed, startTracking() rewrites to direct NodeDB reads, FSM pruned to 6 states

title: On-device crash log persistence (T114)
description: Crash repros only when device is untethered (no serial). Add a RAM ring buffer in RedirectablePrint that periodically flushes recent LOG_* lines to /prefs/crashlog.bin on LittleFS/InternalFS, and dumps the file to serial on next boot so the lead-up to the crash is recoverable.
notes: Implementation landed in PR #37. Pending: flash-and-verify on T114 hardware (repro the untethered crash, confirm [CRASHLOG-BEGIN]...[CRASHLOG-END] dump on next boot). Close once verified.

title: Add Portduino I2C pin macro remap patch
description: GH issue #5 step 1. MagnetometerModule hardcodes I2C0_SDA_PIN/I2C0_SCL_PIN/I2C1_SDA_PIN/I2C1_SCL_PIN (ESP32-Arduino convention); Portduino native env does not define them, so unconditional instantiation at Modules.cpp:168 breaks the native build. Mirror the T114 build-flag remap in patch-t114.py by extending the patcher (or adding patch-native.py) to inject equivalent -DI2C*_{SDA,SCL}_PIN flags into the env:native section of variants/native/portduino/platformio.ini. Blocker for every downstream native-harness task.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #10 (branch native-ci/pin-remap-patch). Adds patch-native.py mirroring T114 pin-remap, plus Power.h case fix. Further native blockers tracked in ff-xwd.

title: Adafruit BluefruitLE nRF51 lib pulled for T114 (nRF52) — err_t conflict
description: Surfaced by the ff-builder devloop. Compile fails with 'conflicting declaration typedef enum err_t err_t' (BluefruitLE nRF51 library errors.h:196) vs 'typedef uint32_t err_t' (framework-arduinoadafruitnrf52 wiring_constants.h:71). The Adafruit BluefruitLE nRF51 library should not be pulled in for an nRF52840 target; it's either a stale lib_deps entry or a transitive dep that needs filtering. Investigation needed: check variants/nrf52840/heltec_mesh_node_t114/platformio.ini and nrf52840_base lib_deps in LeapYeet/firmware. This is upstream firmware config, not a devloop patch-script concern — fix in LeapYeet/firmware directly.
close_reason: ff-hcx: lib_ignore added via patch-t114.py. ff-z6j: make build produces valid firmware.uf2 (1.5 MB, NRF52840, addr 0x026000) in ~50s. Device-flash validation still pending (user), but container devloop is proven end-to-end.

title: Fix case-sensitive include in FriendFinderModule.cpp
description: src/modules/FriendFinderModule.cpp:5 has #include "Power.h" (capital P) but the actual header is src/power.h (lowercase). Case-insensitive filesystems (macOS, Windows) silently resolve this; Linux builds fail with 'Power.h: No such file or directory'. Every other Meshtastic source uses lowercase 'power.h' (main.cpp, Led.cpp, MeshService.cpp, etc.) — this is upstream drift between src/Power.cpp (capital) and src/power.h (lowercase) that FriendFinderModule.cpp aligned with the wrong convention. Fix in devloop: extend patch-t114.py (or rename) to sed -i the include before compile. Real fix: upstream PR to LeapYeet/firmware changing the include to lowercase.
close_reason: Closed

title: Patch T114 build_flags to define I2C{0,1}_{SDA,SCL}_PIN
description: Real cause of CI build failure on heltec-mesh-node-t114: src/modules/MagnetometerModule.h:21-32 falls back to I2C_SDA/I2C_SCL/I2C_SDA1/I2C_SCL1 (ESP32 Arduino convention) when I2C{0,1}_{SDA,SCL}_PIN are not predefined. The T114 nRF52 variant uses Adafruit core convention (PIN_WIRE_SDA = P0.26, PIN_WIRE_SCL = P0.27, PIN_WIRE1_SDA = P0.16, PIN_WIRE1_SCL = P0.13) and never defines I2C_SDA/I2C_SCL, so compile fails with 'I2C_SDA was not declared in this scope'. MagnetometerModule is unconditionally instantiated in Modules.cpp:168 — no board guard. Fix: extend patch-t114.py to inject -DI2C0_SDA_PIN=PIN_WIRE_SDA -DI2C0_SCL_PIN=PIN_WIRE_SCL -DI2C1_SDA_PIN=PIN_WIRE1_SDA -DI2C1_SCL_PIN=PIN_WIRE1_SCL into the T114 variant build_flags, alongside the existing -DSS=0. Same shape as the existing workaround, retirable when upstream fixes MagnetometerModule.h to use PIN_WIRE_SDA on nRF52.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add make targets: dump post-patch source + compile to output/
description:
Two new Make targets using podman + a gitignored output/ dir:

1. patched-src — run patch-t114.py inside the container, copy the post-patch /firmware-src tree (minus .git/.pio) to output/patched-src/ so we can grep/diff the patched C++.
2. compile — same compile path as 'make build' but writes firmware.uf2 to output/ instead of firmware/heltec_t114/. Leave existing 'make build' untouched (CI uses it).

Also: add output/ to .gitignore.
close_reason: Added 'patched-src' and 'compile' Make targets writing to gitignored output/. Dry-run + make help OK. Real end-to-end execution (podman pull + image build) not yet exercised — user will validate.

title: persist Saved Places + magnetometer calibration on nRF52 (gh #48)
description: Sister to issue #25 (friends-list persistence which shipped). On T114/nRF52 the SavedPlace[] array and all magnetometer calibration state (hard-iron bias, scale, soft-iron 2D matrix, userZeroDeg, flipYafterCal, flipNorth) are RAM-only because save*/load* methods are gated behind FF_HAVE_NVS/MAGMOD_HAVE_NVS (= ESP32 only). Follow the same SafeFile + LittleFS pattern the friends-persistence patch already established. Two new patcher blocks: places -> /prefs/places.proto, calibration -> /prefs/magcal.proto. Both versioned binary with magic header, atomic via SafeFile.
close_reason: Patches landed in 2becbf0; podman build green; PR #52 opened. Awaiting field validation: save place + calibrate compass + reboot, confirm both survive.

title: disciplined QMC recovery: safety-net only, no preemptive at boot
description: The existing qmcFailCount + end/begin recovery on PR #44 was helpful as a first attempt but ran too eagerly (3-failure threshold) and the bit-bang variants broke boot detection. With the polling+ODR reductions in place, the failure rate should drop dramatically. New recovery shape: (1) bump threshold to 5 consecutive failures (~1.25s of bad bus at 250ms polling) before recovering; (2) bus recovery is magBus->end() + delay + magBus->begin() + qmcInit — NO bit-bang and NO Wire-side recovery; (3) one recovery attempt per failure cluster — if next read still fails, mark mag disabled until reboot, log loudly, no further attempts; (4) NO preemptive recovery at boot — only after observed failure. Refs gh #43.
close_reason: Landed in single commit 0f1151b on ff-gh43/qmc-low-rate; podman build green; PR #45 opened, supersedes #44. Awaiting TezlaKid retest.

title: lower QMC5883L ODR from 200Hz continuous to 50Hz continuous
description: qmcInit writes CTRL1=0x1D (OSR=512, RNG=8G [comment lies], ODR=200Hz, MODE=continuous). 200Hz continuous means the chip's internal sampling state machine is running all the time — every sample window is an exposure point where an RF glitch can wedge the slave. Drop ODR to 50Hz (CTRL1=0x11): 4× less internal state-machine activity, sample staleness max 20ms (well under any visible latency). Pair with the polling-cadence change. Refs gh #43.
close_reason: Landed in single commit 0f1151b on ff-gh43/qmc-low-rate; podman build green; PR #45 opened, supersedes #44. Awaiting TezlaKid retest.

title: lower QMC polling cadence from 20Hz to ~4Hz
description: Per docs/wire-i2c-timeout-plan.md retrospective on gh #43: patch-and-pray recovery isn't working because the exposure to bus glitches is too high. runOnce returns 50ms on the success path = 20 I2C transactions/sec. The compass UI doesn't need that. Patch the runOnce success return to ~250ms (4Hz) — still snappier than a human can read a heading; proportionally reduces exposure to RF-coincident transactions. Calibration paths (figure-8, flat-spin) should retain higher rate since the user is actively rotating. Refs gh #43.
close_reason: Landed in single commit 0f1151b on ff-gh43/qmc-low-rate; podman build green; PR #45 opened, supersedes #44. Awaiting TezlaKid retest.

title: Rename Treasures -> Saved Places, fix slot display (gh38)
description: Patch MenuHandler.cpp and FriendFinderModule.cpp: rename all 'Treasures'/'treasures' display strings and menu labels to 'Saved Places'. Update menu to always show all MAX_PLACES slots (filled or empty). Empty slots show 'Empty Slot N'. Selecting filled slot starts waypoint nav. Selecting empty slot is a no-op. 'Save Current Location' saves to next empty slot with toast. Remove the standalone 'Save Treasure' entry from the root Compass submenu. See gh issue #38 Change 2.
close_reason: Already satisfied: upstream LeapYeet already uses 'Saved Places', 'Empty Slot N', 'Save Current Location'; no 'Treasure' strings remain. MENU_PLACES_NEW in patch-t114.py covers the empty-slot no-op requirement.

title: Flaky pairing-handshake integration test on Native CI
description: Two-node FriendFinder pairing test in entrypoint-native.sh / tests/smoke/ times out after 60 s with one of the two nodes missing the final two markers ('Received final acceptance from 0x...' and 'Pairing complete'). The missing node alternates between A and B run-to-run, which is the signature of a timing-sensitive handshake that intermittently doesn't complete within the budget rather than a deterministic bug. Confirmed pre-existing on main: scheduled Native CI run 26026122493 (2026-05-18) failed with node A missing those markers on a main HEAD that had no code changes since the 2026-05-11 scheduled run that passed (run 25661961554). PR #37's CI hit the same shape with node B missing instead. The 60 s timeout, the marker scraping, and any deferred-IO between propose/accept/final are the suspects worth investigating first.
acceptance_criteria: Pairing test passes consistently across at least 20 consecutive Native CI runs without code changes.
notes: Update after PR #37 rerun: failure is NOT a clean flake. Main has 1 observed failure (node A missing markers, 5/18 scheduled). My PR has 2 consecutive failures with node B missing the same markers — identical shape, not alternating. Could be sample-size bias or my CrashLog periodic tilting B's timing window. ALSO: the native-smoke-logs artifact uploaded by CI only contains /tmp/ff-smoke-work/ (smoke-phase) logs, not /tmp/ff-pairing-work/ (pairing-phase) logs, so we can't see what pairing-phase node B actually did. Two gaps to close before this is diagnosable: (1) CI artifact upload should include /tmp/ff-pairing-work; (2) local repro with ff-gh8/crashlog-persistence reverted vs applied to A/B-test whether the patch is causally involved.

title: Persist Friends list across power cycles (nRF52/T114)
description: GitHub issue #25: friends list is RAM-only on T114/nRF52 because FriendFinderModule's save/load functions are gated behind '#if defined(ARDUINO_ARCH_ESP32)'. Wire friends persistence into the LittleFS protobuf pattern (NodeDB::saveProto, fullAtomic=true, file at /prefs/friends.proto), shipped as a marker-guarded patch block in patch-t114.py + patch-native.py. Composes with the P0/P1 brick-fix safeToWrite() gate from docs/design/t114-brick-fix.md by routing through the standard saveProto wrapper. Full design lives in OpenSpec change 'persist-friends-list' (openspec/changes/persist-friends-list/) — proposal/design/specs/tasks all validated. Run /opsx:apply when ready to implement.

title: Merge PR #15 (defense-in-depth patches + PR-build CI)
description: Merge PR #15 to main as step 1 of the Patch Landing Order from the merged design doc. Contains: Patch A (FriendFinder reloadConfig -> configChanged.notifyObservers), Patch B (QMC SCL-pulse bus recovery after 3 NAKs), and pr-build-t114.yml CI workflow. Prerequisites: doc correction landed, framing touch-up pushed to PR #15. Use merge-commit strategy (matches project convention visible in recent 'Merge pull request #14' style). Delete branch after merge. Follow-up PRs for P0+P1 (write-policy gate) and P3 (mag poll-rate gating) depend on this landing because they need the CI workflow to produce RC builds for QA.
close_reason: PR #15 merged to main (merge commit 5650b44). Step 1 of Landing Order complete: Patch A, Patch B, and pr-build-t114.yml CI workflow are now on main. Follow-up PRs for P0+P1 and P3 can now produce RC builds via the new CI.

title: Soften PR #15 framing to match design doc
description: PR #15's patch-t114.py docstring (items #5 and #6), the inline comment in the injected ff_qmcBusRecovery helper, and the PR body all still claim the patches fix the brick. The merged design doc is explicit that they are defense-in-depth, not the root-cause fix. Update the framing in three places: (a) patch-t114.py docstring items #5 and #6, (b) the // ff-builder: I2C bus recovery comment block in the injected replacement text, (c) the PR body on GitHub. No code changes. Point readers at docs/design/t114-brick-fix.md for authoritative root-cause framing. Also reflect the post-H2-correction understanding that Patch B now addresses a plausible-but-unobserved path rather than a confirmed one.
close_reason: Framing softened in patch-t114.py docstring items #5/#6 and ff_qmcBusRecovery inline comment; PR #15 body rewritten to frame as Step 1 of the Landing Order per the merged design doc. Pushed to t114-fix/brick-gh12 branch.

title: Correct H2 validation claim in t114-brick-fix design doc
description: The merged design doc (docs/design/t114-brick-fix.md) cites the COM11 log line '[Magnetometer] QMC read failed; will retry.' at uptime 1208s as validation that hypothesis H2 (Wire1 TWIM stall -> watchdog reset) is a confirmed failure path. TezlaKid has since clarified that the log event was user-induced (device physically unplugged during logging), not organic. Update the H2 section and the 'Why PR #15 Is Insufficient' section to restate H2 as plausible-but-unobserved rather than partially-validated. Small correction PR against main.
close_reason: Doc correction merged via PR #17 — H2 restated as plausible-but-unobserved, Root Cause section restructured to differentiate primary vs secondary mechanisms.

title: FriendFinder pairing integration test
description: GH issue #5 step 6. End-to-end integration test exercising FriendFinder's pairing state machine across two simulated native nodes: drive a REQUEST -> ACCEPT handshake and assert both sides transition into TRACKING_TARGET. Satisfies GH issue #5 acceptance criterion 'at least one integration test exercises FriendFinder pairing end-to-end across two simulated nodes'.
close_reason: Pairing integration test shipped in PR #14. Three firmware patches under FF_NATIVE_AUTO_PAIR=1 (runOnce bootstrap, showConfirmationPrompt auto-accept, completePairing screen null-check) plus tests/smoke/pairing_test.py that tails logs for five state-machine markers on both nodes. Required a follow-up stdbuf -oL fix to line-buffer the subprocess stdout (CI run 24737255086 failed on flushing race; 24738062428 green with the fix). Full REQUEST->ACCEPT->complete handshake verified end-to-end on both nodes within first 2s poll.

title: Two-node native smoke test (SimRadio)
description: GH issue #5 step 5. Add a shell script or pytest harness that launches two native 'program' instances with distinct MESHTASTIC_PORT_OFFSET values so SimRadio (UDP loopback) connects them. Use the meshtastic PyPI TCP client to confirm both nodes see each other in NodeDB within a bounded timeout. This is the substrate every higher-level integration test builds on.
close_reason: Two-node SimRadio smoke test shipped in PR #13. USERPREFS_NETWORK_ENABLED_PROTOCOLS=1 enables UDP_BROADCAST by default in installDefaultConfig, so two fresh VFS dirs auto-mesh over 224.0.0.69:4403. Python driver uses meshtastic.tcp_interface to poll NodeDB and assert mutual visibility. Native CI run 24723663092 passed — mutual discovery within one polling cycle. Entrypoint and smoke logs uploaded as CI artifacts.

title: Add ci-native.yml GitHub Actions workflow
description: GH issue #5 step 4. Add a CI workflow on ubuntu-latest that checks out the fork, clones LeapYeet/firmware, applies patches, installs PlatformIO, and runs pio run -e native. Triggers: PRs touching src/modules/FriendFinder* or the patch scripts. Cache ~/.platformio and .pio/build to keep wall time reasonable. Runs alongside (not replacing) the existing T114 release workflow; native CI does NOT need the T114 Containerfile.
close_reason: ci-native.yml runs  on ubuntu-latest by building/running the same Containerfile.native the local dev loop uses. First CI run on PR #10 (run id 24701611492) passed in 6m11s on cold cache, all 5 steps green. Triggers: PR/push paths + weekly cron + workflow_dispatch. Build log uploaded as artifact. Commit 716a554.

title: Fix residual native build failures
description: GH issue #5 step 3. Resolve compile errors surfaced by the pio run -e native attempt. Expected scope: guard unconditional graphics/Screen.h and graphics/draw/* includes in FriendFinderModule.cpp behind HAS_SCREEN; verify MenuHandler.cpp references to friendFinderModule are file-level HAS_SCREEN-gated; add any other small guards needed. Exact scope determined by the build-attempt task output.
close_reason: MagnetometerModule.h gated on ARCH_PORTDUINO with stub class; MagnetometerModule.cpp wrapped under same guard. pio run -e native SUCCESS in 71.79s. Shipped in PR #10 (commit 6ab154b).

title: Attempt clean pio run -e native build with patches applied
description: GH issue #5 step 2. With the Portduino pin-remap patch in place, run pio run -e native against a freshly patched LeapYeet/firmware checkout. Capture any remaining compiler errors (likely: screen header pulls, MenuHandler direct references to friendFinderModule, anything else unguarded). Produces the scope for the follow-up residual-fixes task.
close_reason: Build attempt complete. Findings: (1) libusb-1.0-0-dev needed (ch341 SPI glue), (2) libuv1-dev needed (Portduino AsyncUDP), (3) Adafruit_AHRS.h missing in env:native lib_deps causes MagnetometerModule.h to fail compilation in MenuHandler.cpp. (1) and (2) added to Containerfile.native in commit 94a0dc3. (3) and the deeper magnetometer issues (Wire1 / Adafruit_LIS3DH) tracked in ff-5k6.

title: Automate T114 releases on push to main (CalVer, single workflow)
description: Replace .github/workflows/build-t114.yml with .github/workflows/release.yml that fires on push to main, builds the T114 firmware via the ff-builder container, and publishes a prerelease with CalVer tag v<YYYY>.<MM>.<DD>-<run_number>, auto-generated release notes, and firmware.uf2 attached. Single workflow, no manual dispatch path, no reusable-workflow refactor. Closes GH #7.
close_reason: Stale. Work shipped 2026-04-17 via PR #9 (commit ac60d0d on main). release.yml fires on push to main, builds T114 firmware via ff-builder image, publishes a CalVer-tagged prerelease with firmware.uf2 attached. GH issue #7 closed at same time. Bead was never closed at merge; closing now.

title: Add LICENSE file + README license section (GH issue #6)
description: Minimal GPL-3.0 compliance per GitHub issue #6. Drop verbatim GPL-3 text at repo root and add a License section to README citing derivation chain from LeapYeet/Meshtastic-Firmware-Friend-Finder-Edition and meshtastic/firmware, both GPL-3. No SPDX headers, no per-file copyright notices (deferred as optional niceties).
close_reason: PR #8 opened; GH issue #6 will close on merge

title: CI: build image from Containerfile and one-shot the UF2 to release
description: Instead of duplicating PlatformIO setup and patch logic in the workflow, have CI just 'docker build + docker run' the Containerfile in this repo and upload /output/firmware.uf2 to the release. The image already knows how to clone LeapYeet/firmware and bake the toolchain; make that clone the permanent /firmware-src rather than a throwaway seed. Local devloop keeps working via bind-mount override.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add Makefile wrapper for ff-builder devloop
description: User wants a single 'make build' entry point instead of remembering ./build.sh and the podman build incantation. Targets: setup (clone LeapYeet/firmware if missing), image (podman build ff-builder if missing), rebuild (--no-cache force), build (setup + image + run), shell (interactive container), clean (rm artifact), help. Follow CLAUDE.md convention: Makefile as primary automation interface.
close_reason: Closed

title: Validate local ff-builder produces working firmware.uf2
description: Once-off validation: clone LeapYeet/firmware outside the repo, podman build -t ff-builder ., run ./build.sh, confirm firmware/heltec_t114/firmware.uf2 is produced and byte-compatible with the CI-produced artifact for the same source SHA (sha256sum compare). If divergence, investigate before declaring parity.
close_reason: ff-hcx: lib_ignore added via patch-t114.py. ff-z6j: make build produces valid firmware.uf2 (1.5 MB, NRF52840, addr 0x026000) in ~50s. Device-flash validation still pending (user), but container devloop is proven end-to-end.

title: Write Containerfile for ff-builder image
description: Ubuntu 24.04 base; installs PlatformIO in a venv; pre-bakes the nRF52 platform + ARM GCC toolchain by doing a throwaway LeapYeet/firmware clone and running pio pkg install --environment heltec-mesh-node-t114; COPYs patch-t114.py + entrypoint.sh. Target arch linux/amd64 (host-native). See docs/design/containerized-devloop.md #containerfile.
close_reason: Closed

title: Write build.sh local Podman wrapper
description: Wrapper that bind-mounts $FIRMWARE_SRC (default ~/code-stuff/LeapYeet-firmware) to /firmware-src and firmware/heltec_t114 to /output, then runs ff-builder. Uses :Z mount label for SELinux compatibility. Errors loudly if the firmware source clone is missing. See docs/design/containerized-devloop.md #buildsh.
close_reason: Closed

title: Write entrypoint.sh for ff-builder container
description: Container entrypoint that runs patch-t114.py, invokes pio run --environment heltec-mesh-node-t114, and copies .pio/build/heltec-mesh-node-t114/firmware.uf2 to /output/firmware.uf2. See docs/design/containerized-devloop.md #entrypointsh.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract patch-t114.py from workflow
description: The current GHA embeds an inline Python heredoc that patches variants/nrf52840/heltec_mesh_node_t114/platformio.ini to add -DSS=0. Extract this into a standalone patch-t114.py at repo root so both the container and CI can use identical logic. See docs/design/containerized-devloop.md #patch-t114py.
close_reason: Closed

title: remove 'Track a Friend' and 'Dev Tools' from friendFinderBaseMenu
description: User request: remove two items from the Captain Compass (formerly Friend Finder) menu. After removal the menu is: Back / Saved Places / Compass Cal. Need to also renumber the callback selected==N branches in lockstep. The underlying handlers (friend_finder_list_menu, friend_finder_dev_tools_menu) stay — they're reachable from elsewhere (favorites menu) or dead-code that's not in scope here.
close_reason: Patch landed; build green; PR opened.

title: rename 'Friend Finder' menu label to 'Captain Compass'
description: User-visible menu strings only: optionsArray[options] = "Friend Finder" (home menu, hoisted by ordering patch); optionsArray[options] = "Track (Friend Finder)" (favorites menu, added by compass redesign); bannerOptions.message = "Friend Finder" (banner title). Internal C++ identifiers (FriendFinderModule, friendFinderModule, file paths) unchanged — those aren't user-visible.
close_reason: Patch landed; podman build green; PR opened.

title: Document native dev loop in README
description: GH issue #5 step 7. Add a short README section on running the native Portduino harness locally: how to clone LeapYeet/firmware, apply patches, build via pio run -e native, and launch two program instances with MESHTASTIC_PORT_OFFSET for FriendFinder development. Mirrors existing T114 build/flash instructions in style. Satisfies GH issue #5 acceptance criterion on README documentation.
close_reason: Added Local Development section to README covering Containerfile.native quick start, host bind-mount, interactive shell escape hatch, what patch-native.py does, and simulator limitations. Commit 73501b7 in PR #10.

title: Stop committing firmware.uf2 to repo; release asset is canonical
description: The containerized devloop writes firmware/heltec_t114/firmware.uf2 on every build, polluting git status. Gitignore the path; the build-t114.yml release workflow already does 'git add firmware/heltec_t114/firmware.uf2' — update that to 'git add -f ...' so the release pipeline can still commit the artifact.
notes: Scope clarified: GitHub Pages is not enabled on soniccyclone/... (verified via gh api). index.html is vestigial fork baggage — only LeapYeet's Pages serves a flasher, and it serves LeapYeet's binary, not ours. Drop the 'Commit UF2 to flasher repo' step entirely; gitignore firmware/heltec_t114/firmware.uf2 for local builds; fix release body text that advertises the LeapYeet web flasher as serving this fork's UF2.
close_reason: Closed

title: Switch build-t114.yml to run inside ff-builder container
description: Update .github/workflows/build-t114.yml to use the published ghcr.io/<owner>/ff-builder:latest as a job container, dropping the per-run toolchain install and the inline Python patch. Keep the existing checkout of LeapYeet/firmware, the artifact copy into flasher repo, and the gh-release step unchanged.

title: Publish ff-builder image to GHCR on Containerfile changes
description: Add a separate workflow that builds the ff-builder image and pushes to ghcr.io/<owner>/ff-builder:latest when Containerfile, patch-t114.py, or entrypoint.sh change on main. This is the prerequisite for switching build-t114.yml to run inside the container (Option B in the design doc).
notes: GHCR owner is soniccyclone — image path is ghcr.io/soniccyclone/ff-builder. Fork owner explicitly wants no automation reaching back to upstream LeapYeet/Meshtastic, so all image/release namespaces stay under soniccyclone/.

value: GHCR images for this fork (Meshtastic-Firmware-Friend-Finder-Edition) publish under ghcr.io/soniccyclone/. Owner explicitly does NOT want any workflow, image, or automation reaching back to the upstream repo (LeapYeet or the original Meshtastic project) — keep all CI artifacts, releases, and image namespaces under soniccyclone/.
